The patch is written to `/workspace/repo/patch.txt`. Let me do a final check of the inbox for any coordination needed from agent2 before wrapping up.

Implementation complete. Summary:

- Added an optional `repeat: usize` named parameter (default `1`) to `str.first()` and `str.last()` in `crates/typst/src/foundations/str.rs`.
- `first(repeat: n)` collects the first `n` grapheme clusters; `last(repeat: n)` collects the last `n` (in original order), both fully Unicode-aware via `unicode-segmentation`.
- Preserves backward compatibility: default `repeat: 1` behaves exactly as before, including the `"string is empty"` error text for empty strings (verified against existing tests in `tests/suite/foundations/str.typ`).
- Added a new `not_enough_graphemes` error helper for the case where `repeat` exceeds the available grapheme count on a non-empty string.
- No other call sites in the crate invoke `Str::first`/`Str::last` from Rust, so the signature change is safe.
- Notified agent2 (also editing `first`/`last` in the same file for a `strip` param) about the potential conflict; no response/edits landed on my side, and my diff only touches the `first`/`last` methods plus the new error helper.

Diff written to `/workspace/repo/patch.txt`.

result:
Implementation complete. Summary:

- Added an optional `repeat: usize` named parameter (default `1`) to `str.first()` and `str.last()` in `crates/typst/src/foundations/str.rs`.
- `first(repeat: n)` collects the first `n` grapheme clusters; `last(repeat: n)` collects the last `n` (in original order), both fully Unicode-aware via `unicode-segmentation`.
- Preserves backward compatibility: default `repeat: 1` behaves exactly as before, including the `"string is empty"` error text for empty strings (verified against existing tests in `tests/suite/foundations/str.typ`).
- Added a new `not_enough_graphemes` error helper for the case where `repeat` exceeds the available grapheme count on a non-empty string.
- No other call sites in the crate invoke `Str::first`/`Str::last` from Rust, so the signature change is safe.
- Notified agent2 (also editing `first`/`last` in the same file for a `strip` param) about the potential conflict; no response/edits landed on my side, and my diff only touches the `first`/`last` methods plus the new error helper.

Diff written to `/workspace/repo/patch.txt`.
